title: Order by disorder up to arbitrarily high temperature
abstract: We prove that a class of classical lattice models on $\mathbb{Z}^d$ ($d \geq 2$) with on-site space $\mathbb{N}_0$ exhibits long-range checkerboard order at sufficiently high temperature. The model has a nearest-neighbour interaction $f : \mathbb{N}_0 \times \mathbb{N}_0 \to [0,\infty)$ satisfying four structural conditions, subsuming the recently introduced power-law model of Han--Huang--Komargodski--Lucas--Popov (arXiv:2503.22789) as a special case. The ordering mechanism is purely entropic: the checkerboard configurations are not energy minimisers, but are selected by the partial trace over occupation numbers in the $β\to 0$ limit. The proof uses Pirogov--Sinai theory and the key input is a Peierls bound.
summary: This paper proves that certain classical lattice models exhibit long-range checkerboard order at arbitrarily high temperatures. This phenomenon, known as 'order by disorder,' occurs because thermal fluctuations (entropy) select specific ordered patterns even though they are not the energy-minimizing ground states. The result is established using rigorous mathematical tools like Pirogov-Sinai theory.
main_problem: Proving the existence of long-range checkerboard order in a class of classical lattice models at arbitrarily high temperatures, where the ordering is driven by entropy rather than energy minimization.
main_result: The author proves that for sufficiently small inverse temperature (high temperature), the Gibbs measure is concentrated near checkerboard configurations, establishing the existence of distinct infinite-volume Gibbs states.
method: The proof utilizes Pirogov-Sinai theory, cluster expansion, and a polymer representation of the partition function, supported by a Peierls-type bound on contour surface energy.
model_or_system: A classical lattice model on a d-dimensional hypercubic lattice (d >= 2) with an unbounded on-site state space (bosonic occupation numbers) and a nearest-neighbor interaction function satisfying specific symmetry and growth conditions.
key_observables: The difference between the Gibbs measure of occupation labels and the reference checkerboard configurations; the existence of distinct infinite-volume Gibbs states.
important_parameters: Inverse temperature (beta) in the limit beta -> 0; on-site energy scale (U); growth exponent (alpha).
main_assumptions: The proof relies on the validity of the contour weight bound and the existence of a polymer representation within the high-temperature regime.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates a configuration with two contours, showing the boundary of a macro-site.
paper_structure: The paper identifies the model and its interaction conditions, constructs a macro-lattice to define reference configurations, derives a polymer representation of the partition function, establishes Peierls-type bounds on contour energy, and concludes with the proof of phase coexistence and uniform convergence to the checkerboard states.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: A configuration with two contours γ1 and γ2 with the boundary Bpωq denoted by the dark grey region and the patterned regions representing boundaries of the contours. The outer boundary has the phase η1 and the interior boundary has the same phase as that of the interior components along which they are drawn.
